Weigh and transfer about 5.8 g of Ammonium dihydrogen phosphate in 1000 mL of Milli-Q- water.

preparations.preparation_text:

preparations.section_name: Standard stock solution for Empagliflozin preparation
preparations.preparation_text:
Weigh and transfer about 55.6 mg of Empagliflozin standard into a 50 mL of volumetric flask.
Add about 30 mL of Methanol, sonicate for about 5 minutes to dissolve, and dilute to volume with Methanol.

Pipette 5 mL of standard stock solution for Alogliptin, 2 mL of standard stock solution for Empagliflozin and 3 mL of methanol into 200 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with pH 6.8 dissolution medium and mix well.

Transfer 900 mL dissolution medium into each dissolution bowl. Drop one tablet into each bow, run the dissolution as per the dissolution parameters.
Withdraw sufficient volume of the sample aliquot from each bowl at 37 °C. Filter the sample solution through 0.45 µm PVDF syringe filter.
Note: After 15 minutes of sampling, replace with the same volume of the dissolution medium maintained at 37°C ± 0.5 °C at each specified time interval.
Dissolution Parameters
Chromatographic System
Procedure
Inject the following solutions as mentioned below.
Blank solution can be injected more for stabilization.
Prepare and inject one vial for standard and bracketing standard.
System Suitability
The tailing factor of Alogliptin peak and Empagliflozin peak obtained from standard solution chromatogram should not be more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Alogliptin peak and Empagliflozin peak from the replicate standard solution injections should not be more than 2.0 %.
The relative standard deviation for peak areas of Alogliptin peak and Empagliflozin peak from the replicate standard solution and bracketing standard solution injections should not be more than 2.0 %.
Calculation
AS	: Average peak area of Alogliptin peak in the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Alogliptin in the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of standard taken in mg as Alogliptin benzoate.
P	: Potency of the standard as Alogliptin benzoate, on as is basis
L	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Alogliptin
339.39	: The molecular weight of Alogliptin.
461.51	: The molecular weight of Alogliptin benzoate.
AS	: Average peak area of Empagliflozin peak in the standard solution
AT	: Peak area of Empagliflozin in the sample solution
Ws	: Weight of standard taken in mg as Empagliflozin
P	: Potency of the standard as Empagliflozin, on as is basis
L	: Label claim of the tablets in mg as Empagliflozin
Calculation of Correction factors
Note: Here “V” is sampled volume and “D” is dissolution rate.
% Empagliflozin present in sampled volume after 1st time interval,
F1 = (D1/900) × V
Calculation of Corrected results
For 1st time interval = D1 (No correction required).
For 2nd time interval = D2 + F1
Note: Continue testing through the three levels unless the results confirm at either S1 or S2.
*Q: Quantity, the amount of dissolved active ingredient specified in the individual monograph, expressed as a percentage of the labeled content.

preparations.preparation_text:
Put about 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and distribute 1 mL of prepared 1:10 sample solution to each petri dishes.
Pour 15-20 mL of TSA to each 2-petri dish with sample solution dispensed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample solution with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days. (Total 7 days)
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and distribute 1 mL of prepared 1:10 sample solution to each petri dishes.
Pour 15-20 mL of SDA to each 2-petri dish with sample solution dispensed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample solution with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform the TAMC test and TYMC test using diluent without sample in the same manner.

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample preparation method can be used.
Put about 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Pre-incubation
Put 10mL of sample solution into 90 mL of TSB.
Incubate the prepared solution at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container and transfer 1 mL of incubated TSB to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E. coli. This is confirmed by identification test, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether.
Negative Control Test
Perform the E. coli test putting 10mL of SCPB into the 90mL TSB without sample in the same manner.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Staphylococcus aureus (S.aureus)
Test Method
Test method is selection culture method of S.aureus using selective Media and confirmatory Media (MSA).

Note: It can possible to use the diluent and sample solution prepared in microbial enumeration test.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Put 10mL(Dilution factor 1:10) of sample solution into 90 mL of TSB.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 18 - 24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Streak the incubated TSB on the surface of MSA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 18 - 72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MSA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of S.aureus. This is confirmed by identification test, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether.
Negative Control Test
Perform the S.aureus test putting 10mL of SCPB into the 90mL TSB without sample in the same manner.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Pseudomonas aeruginosa (P.aeruginosa)
Test Method
Test method is selection culture method of P.aeruginosa using selective Media and confirmatory Media (CMA).

Note: It can possible to use the diluent and sample solution prepared in microbial enumeration test.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of SCPB and mix the sample to suspension.
Put 10mL(Dilution factor 1:10) of sample solution into 90 mL of TSB.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 18 - 24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Streak the incubated TSB on the surface of CMA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 18 - 72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours as per (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of CMA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of P.aeruginosa. This is confirmed by identification test, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether.
Negative Control Test
Perform the P.aeruginosa test putting 10mL of SCPB into the 90mL TSB without sample in the same manner.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Salmonella Typhimurium (S.Typhimurium)
Test Method
Test method is selection culture method of S.Typhimurium using selective Media (RVSE broth) and confirmatory Media (XLD).

Put not less than 10 g of sample into the 290 mL of TSB. And then, stir the sample to suspension.
Incubate at 30 – 35 ˚C for 18 - 24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the incubated TSB and then Transfer 0.1 mL to 10 mL of RVSE.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 18 - 24 hours.
Streak incubated RVSE broth on the surface of XLD using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 18 - 48 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 72 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of XLD.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of S.Typhimurium.. This is confirmed by identification test, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether.
Negative Control Test
Perform the S.Typhimurium test putting 10mL of SCPB into the 90mL TSB without sample in the same manner.
N-Nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA)
Note: This item is limited to outsourced testing conducted exclusively for the Korean market.
Dissolve NDMA-d6, internal standard, in methanol with a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Dissolve NDMA standard solution in methanol to achieve a concentration of 1 μg/mL.
Mix the standard solution and internal standard solution in the amounts specified in the table below and dilute to 10 mL with dichloromethane accurately.

Accurately weigh not less than 20 tablets and finely powder. Accurately weigh equivalent to 500 mg of Metformin Hydrochloride, add 5 mL of 1 mol/L hydrochloric acid, then accurately take 50 μL of the internal standard solution and shake thoroughly to mix.
Add 5 mL of dichloromethane to this solution accurately, shake thoroughly to mix, and then centrifuge at 4,000 rpm for 10 minutes.1) Carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer, filter it through a 0.22 μm membrane filter, discard the first filtrate, and use the subsequent filtrate as the sample solution.
1) If necessary, carefully take an appropriate amount of the dichloromethane layer and further centrifuge at 13,000 rpm for 10 minutes.
GC-MS/MS operating condition
System suitability
The coefficient of determination (R2) of the calibration curve should be not less than 0.998.
The S/N ratio at a standard solution concentration of 1 ng/mL should be not less than 10.
The tailing factor of NDMA peak at a standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL should be not more than 2.0.
The relative standard deviation of the NDMA peak area to the internal standard peak area for standard solution concentration of 5 ng/mL six replicate injection should be not more than 5.0 %.

에제티미브 표준원액, 로수바스타틴 표준원액 및 암로디핀 표준원액을 각각 10 mL, 20 mL 및 10 mL를 정확하게 취하여 100 mL 차광 용량 플라스크에 넣고 희석액으로 표선을 맞추어 표준액으로 한다.

이 약 20정을 1000 mL 차광 용량 플라스크에 넣고 희석액 약 700 mL를 가한 후 마그네틱바를 넣고 30분간 교반하여 녹인다.
정제가 완전히 붕해되면 마그네틱바를 꺼내고 30분간 초음파 추출한 후 실온에서 방냉한다. (초음파 추출 시 검액을 흔들어준다.) 희석액을 넣어 정확히 표선을 맞추고, 잘 섞어준 다음 4000 rpm으로 20분간 원심분리 한다.
상등액 5 mL를 정확히 취하여 20 mL 차광용량플라스크에 넣고 희석액을 넣어 잘 혼화하여 표선을 맞춘다. 이 액을 0.45 µm PTFE 멤브레인 필터 (Whatman)로 여과하여 검액으로 한다.

preparations.preparation_text: Dissolve about 2.0 g of sodium chloride and 7.0 mL of hydrochloric acid in 1000 mL of purified water and then adjust the pH to 1.2 ± 0.05.

Weigh and transfer about 3.45 g of potassium phosphate monobasic into 1000 mL of Milli-Q- water. Adjust the pH 3.0± 0.02 with ortho-phosphoric acid.
Mix buffer and acetonitrile in the ratio of 680 : 320 v/v, respectively. Degas in a sonicator for about 10 minutes.

preparations.section_name: Standard stock solution for Pioglitazone preparation
preparations.preparation_text:
Weigh and transfer about 61.0 mg of Pioglitazone hydrochloride standard into a 50 mL of volumetric flask.
Add 30 mL of methanol, sonicate for 10 minutes to dissolve, and dilute to volume with methanol.

preparations.section_name: Standard stock solution for Empagliflozin preparation
preparations.preparation_text:
Weigh and transfer about 70.0 mg of Empagliflozin standard into a 50 mL of volumetric flask.
Add 30 mL of methanol, sonicate for 10 minutes to dissolve, and dilute to volume with methanol.

Pipette 6 mL of standard stock solution for Pioglitazone and 4 mL of standard stock solution for Empagliflozin into 200 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with pH 1.2 dissolution medium and mix well.

Pipette 6 mL of standard stock solution for Pioglitazone, 2 mL of standard stock solution for Empagliflozin and 2 mL of methanol into 200 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with pH 1.2 dissolution medium and mix well.

Pipette 3 mL of standard stock solution for Pioglitazone, 4 mL of standard stock solution for Empagliflozin and 3 mL of methanol into 200 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with pH 1.2 dissolution medium and mix well.

Pipette 3 mL of standard stock solution for Pioglitazone, 2 mL of standard stock solution for Empagliflozin and 5 mL of methanol into 200 mL volumetric flask and dilute to volume with pH 1.2 dissolution medium and mix well.

Transfer 900 mL dissolution medium into each dissolution bowl. Drop one tablet into each bow, run the dissolution as per the dissolution parameters.
Withdraw sufficient volume of the sample aliquot from each bowl at 37 °C. Filter the sample solution through 0.45 µm PVDF syringe filter (Millipore) or 0.45 µm Nylon syringe filter (Millipore).

Total Aerobic Microbial Count (TAMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:50) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of TSA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 30 - 35 ˚C for 5 days.
Perform interim colony counting on days 2-4 and final colony counting on days 5 of incubation.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 2 days (Total 7 days).
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 50 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Total Combined Yeasts and Molds Count (TYMC) Test
Place 2 petri dishes and transfer 1 mL of sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) into each petri dish.
Pour 15 ~ 20 mL of SDA to each petri dish with sample solution dispersed. Swirl the petri dishes slowly to mix sample with media.
Incubate at 20 - 25 ˚C for 7 days.
Perform Interim colony counting on days 3-5 and final colony counting on days 7 of incubation.
After incubation, resulting value is calculated by averaging the values obtained by multiplying dilution rate 10 to colony number detected from each petri dish.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests using diluent without sample in the same as the above procedure.
Specified Microorganisms Test: Escherichia coli (E.coli)
Media
Test Method
Note: Test method is selection culture method of E. coli using selective media (MCB) and confirmatory medium (MCA).

Note: Sample solution (Dilution factor 1:10) of microbial enumeration test that is the same sample solution method can be used.
Put 10 g of sample into 90 mL of diluent and mix it to homogenize.
Pre-incubation
Transfer 10 mL of sample solution into the 90 mL of TSB. After that, mix the sample to homogenize.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-24 hours.
Selection and Subculture
Shake the container, and transfer 1 mL of incubated TSB to 100 mL MCB.
Incubate at 42-44 ℃ for 24-48 hours.
Streak incubated MCB on the surface of MCA using inoculating loop.
Incubate at 30-35 ℃ for 18-72 hours.
Note: It can be possible to incubate additionally 48 hours (Total 120 hours).
After incubation, confirm the growth of colonies on the surface of MCA.
Growth of colonies indicates the possible presence of E.coli, This is confirmed by identification tests, and follow the QS09004 Microbial Impact Assessment Procedure to determine whether MIA is required.
Negative Control Test
Perform tests without sample in the same as the above procedure.
